behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: The main failure boundary is wiring: source clients, aggregation, and handoff to the summarizer interface. A unit test on a single combiner function can miss real mapping/serialization mismatches between modules. Real network or subprocess behavior is not required to catch this specific failure.
behaviors.test_description: Run the report assembly pipeline with test doubles for the three source adapters (each returning distinct sentinel fields) and assert the exact payload sent to the summary-generation boundary contains all three sections/keys. Use real module wiring, not a single-function unit.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is resilience across modules (collector error handling, section rendering, pipeline continuation). Pure unit mocks can miss exception propagation and fallback formatting at module boundaries. No real external infra is strictly needed if failure is injected at the adapter boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the report pipeline with two source adapters returning valid data and one adapter raising a timeout/error. Verify the run completes, output includes `data unavailable` only for the failed section, and other sections are preserved.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: If summary generation uses a real LLM, correctness depends on non-deterministic model output (possible omission, distortion, or hallucination). Mocking the model would systematically hide these real failures.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real summary-generation model with fixed structured inputs containing clear key values, run multiple trials, and score outputs against assertions for factual inclusion/consistency (e.g., required numeric facts must match source values). Fail if fidelity drops below threshold.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary includes real HTTP/API calls plus SMTP/email provider behavior and inbox delivery. In-process mocks can pass while real auth, DNS, TLS, routing, or provider-side formatting failures break delivery.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end job against reachable test instances/services (or staging equivalents), using a real recipient mailbox. Verify the email is received in inbox with expected subject/body/attachments and reflects the collected data for that run.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real failure modes of the email transport plus durable local persistence and cross-run retry behavior. Mocking email transport can hide protocol/connectivity edge cases; mocking scheduling/run boundaries can miss retry-state bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Trigger one scheduled run while email service endpoint is actually unreachable (e.g., blocked/down test endpoint). Assert report is persisted to local fallback path. On the next scheduled run with service restored, assert the persisted report is retried and successfully sent, then marked cleared.
